examples: The report condemns America's scattershot approach to training workers.
But the distribution of food was scattershot, and every outlet was swamped with desperate crowds.
The policy-related passages in “The Audacity of Hope”, his second autobiography, are scattershot and banal.
Her offensives were often scattershot, appearing at one rally and disappearing at the next.
The considerable spell the movie casts is weakened by its length and sometimes scattershot approach.
